why_winner: Hook B ('Solo developers are a bottleneck. Agent teams are the fix.') is the sharpest scroll-stopper — contrarian and specific, not vague. The comment-bait CTA ('what would YOU give them to build?') drives more engagement than A's save-bait or C's follow-bait; for a hot-take reel this is the right play. B also already incorporates tick-by-tick proof beats (borrowed from the Demo angle) giving it both a strong claim AND demonstrated evidence — the best of both worlds.

length_check.C: borderline (~35s) — on-camera story angle justifies it; follow-bait CTA is the weakest link

runner_up_notes.A: Save-bait CTA ('Save this — next: how to build your own team') is excellent for tutorial-mode content. Worth using on a future Direct reel with a how-to format. The tick-by-tick overlay structure is already present in B.
runner_up_notes.C: The 'I walked away' emotional beat is the most human moment across all three scripts. Could work well as a standalone story reel in a future run if on-camera filming is available.

issues.description: Beat [22-28s] in B — 'If they can close a full task now — what's next?' — is a rhetorical open loop held for 6s without a concrete tease. Slight drop risk before the CTA.
issues.action: On any future revision: trim to 3s or add a one-line concrete tease ('Think: entire products, from a brief'). Not blocking approval — no hard fail present.
